echo "===== CLUSTER STATUS ====="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl cluster_status 2>/dev/null | head -40
echo "===== VHOSTS ====="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_vhosts 2>/dev/null
echo "===== USERS ====="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_users 2>/dev/null

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-maximal-lifecycle-roll-af2b323e-cluster-a
for vh in / /app /ops; do
echo "===== PERMISSIONS vhost=$vh ====="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p "$vh" 2>/dev/null
echo "===== POLICIES vhost=$vh ====="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_policies -p "$vh" 2>/dev/null
echo "===== QUEUES vhost=$vh ====="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_queues -p "$vh" name type durable arguments 2>/dev/null
echo "===== EXCHANGES vhost=$vh ====="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_exchanges -p "$vh" name type 2>/dev/null | grep -v "amq\.\|^name\|^$\|^Listing"
done

content.text: I have the full picture. Let me grab feature flags (critical for upgrade planning) and write the plan.

input.command:
NS=karma-rabbitmq-maximal-lifecycle-roll-af2b323e-cluster-a
echo "===== FEATURE FLAGS ====="
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_feature_flags name state 2>/dev/null
echo "===== STS update strategy / replicas ====="
kubectl -n $NS get sts rabbitmq -o jsonpath='replicas={.spec.replicas} strategy={.spec.updateStrategy.type} podMgmt={.spec.podManagementPolicy}'; echo
echo "===== PVCs ====="
kubectl -n $NS get pvc 2>/dev/null
